# Learnings

- Runtime services are defined under `src\DeskQuotes\Services\Implementations`; tests should import that namespace rather than the removed legacy `DeskQuotes.Services` duplicates to avoid ambiguous type references.
- Use the root `run-local.ps1` script for local workflows: `app`, `tests`, `unit-tests`, and `e2e-tests`. If no E2E project exists yet, the `e2e-tests` target prints a skip message and exits successfully.
- The wallpaper refresh hotkey and edit settings hotkey are both implemented via `GlobalHotkeyService`, which combines Win32 `RegisterHotKey` with a WinForms `IMessageFilter` because `TrayAppContext` is an `ApplicationContext` rather than a form with its own window handle. The service supports multiple hotkeys via a `Dictionary<int, Action>` keyed by hotkey ID; the message filter is registered once and shared across all hotkeys.
- `WallpaperRenderService` is registered as a singleton and now rotates through a curated dark background palette, so consecutive wallpaper refreshes use different dark colors without changing the output file path.
- `WallpaperBackgroundColorService` now owns wallpaper background state: normal refreshes still auto-pick from the dark palette, while tray/hotkey color actions pass an explicit background color for an immediate one-off refresh and then future normal refreshes continue auto-picking dark colors.
- Quote font selection now follows the same stateful pattern as quote/background handling: standard refreshes pick a random font from a curated Windows-safe list, while background-color-only refreshes reuse the current quote and current font so palette tweaks do not unexpectedly restyle the wallpaper.
- The `Change Wallpaper Font > Random Font (Ctrl + Alt + F)` tray/hotkey action is implemented via `WallpaperUpdateService.TryUpdateWallpaperWithRandomFont`, which preserves the current quote/background and always switches to a different curated font.
- `tests\DeskQuotes.UnitTests` now uses xUnit v3 packages and `Assert`/`Record` APIs for assertions; do not add `FluentAssertions` back into the unit test project.
- Quote configuration now supports an optional `Tags` property plus a top-level `tagCatalog` in `settings.json`; `Set Mood` uses those tags for quote eligibility, so each mood-filterable quote should tag itself with values drawn from `tagCatalog`.
- Selected mood state is persisted separately from `settings.json` under `%LocalAppData%\DeskQuotes\selected-mood.txt`; the tray app binds `tagCatalog` in configured order to build the `Set Mood` submenu, and wallpaper refreshes keep the current wallpaper with a warning when the selected mood has no matching tagged quotes.
